plan: Use the clue "reacts with water at high temperatures to form a binary compound B" to generate candidate elements for A.
Apply the constraints on compound C (ternary, two metals in 1:1 ratio, strong oxidizer, no water of crystallisation, formula mass > 230, 74.6% A by mass) to identify A and C.
Calculate the formula mass from the percentage and check candidate formulas.
Verify the balanced reaction from B to C using concentrated NaOH + Br₂.

Confirm all five stated properties of C are satisfied. --- ## Solution ### Step 1 – Identify candidate elements for A Many metals react with steam at high temperature: Fe, Bi, Sn, Pb, etc. ### Step 2 – Use the percentage mass constraint to narrow candidates Let compound C have the formula **M₁A₁O_n** (one atom of each metal, since the ratio is 1:1). $$\frac{M_A}{M_A + M_M + 16n} = 0.746$$ Trying **A = Bi (208.98)** and **M = Na (22.99)**: $$\frac{208.98}{208.98 + 22.99 + 16n} = 0.746 \implies 16n = \frac{208.98}{0.746} - 231.97 = 279.99 - 231.97 \approx 48$$ $$n = 3 \quad \Rightarrow \quad \text{Formula: } \mathbf{NaBiO_3}$$ ### Step 3 – Check all conditions for NaBiO₃ | Property | Check | |---|---| | Formula mass | 22.99 + 208.98 + 3(16.00) = **280.0 > 230** ✓ | | % Bi by mass | 208.98 / 280.0 = **74.6%** ✓ | | Two metals in 1:1 ratio | Na : Bi = 1 : 1 ✓ | | Ternary compound | Na, Bi, O — three elements ✓ | | No water of crystallisation | NaBiO₃ is anhydrous ✓ | | Strong oxidiser | Bi(V) in NaBiO₃ is a powerful oxidant ✓ | ### Step 4 – Verify the reaction pathway **A → B (high-temperature reaction with water):** $$2\text{Bi} + 3\text{H}_2\text{O} \xrightarrow{\Delta} \text{Bi}_2\text{O}_3 + 3\text{H}_2$$ Binary compound B = **Bi₂O₃** ✓ **B → C (concentrated NaOH + Br₂, oxidising Bi(III) → Bi(V)):** $$\text{Bi}_2\text{O}_3 + 2\text{Br}_2 + 6\text{NaOH} \rightarrow 2\text{NaBiO}_3 + 4\text{NaBr} + 3\text{H}_2\text{O}$$ Atom balance: Bi 2=2 ✓, O 9=9 ✓, Na 6=6 ✓, Br 4=4 ✓, H 6=6 ✓ --- **Conclusion:** * **A** = Bismuth (Bi) * **B** = Bi₂O₃ (bismuth(III) oxide) * **C** = **NaBiO₃** (sodium bismuthate) FINAL ANSWER NaBiO₃

trajectory.thought: The plan already contains the complete solution. Element A reacts with water at high temperatures to form a binary compound B. The key candidates are metals that react with steam. Bismuth (Bi) reacts with steam at high temperatures to form Bi₂O₃ (binary compound B). The plan has already worked through all the constraints and verified the answer.
The calculation has already been completed in the plan. For NaBiO₃:
The balanced reaction from B (Bi₂O₃) to C (NaBiO₃) using concentrated NaOH and Br₂ is:

All five properties of NaBiO₃ are confirmed: (1) ternary compound (Na, Bi, O), (2) two metals Na and Bi in 1:1 ratio, (3) strong oxidiser due to Bi(V), (4) no water of crystallisation, (5) formula mass 280.0 > 230, and % Bi = 208.98/280.0 = 74.6%. The reaction pathway is verified with balanced equations.
